name: Soy “sauerbraten
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine TVP® chunks, ketchup, and water in a bowl. Stir until the ketchup is fully incorporated.
2. Let the mixture soak for 5 minutes.
3. Add an additional cup of hot water to the bowl and stir gently.
4. Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat on top of the stove and cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until tender.
5. In a separate saucepan, combine vinegar, 1/2 cup water, honey, bay leaf, and whole cloves. Heat over medium-high heat until it comes to a boil.
6. Remove from heat and add the TVP® chunks. Let marinate overnight in the refrigerator.
7. The next day, remove the TVP® chunks and discard them. Drain the marinade, discarding the cloves.
8. In a large bowl, mix together cornstarch, soy sauce, and soy sour cream until well combined.
9. Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add the TVP® chunks (if using) to the hot oil and cook until heated through, about 2-3 minutes.
10. Pour in the marinade mixture and stir gently to combine with the meat pieces.
11. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes until heated through.
12. Serve over noodles.
VT February 1992
Posted by Mark Satterly
